title: Anthropic launches Claude Corps with a $150M commitment for AI fellowships
friendlyTitle: Anthropic funds a fellowship to bring Claude into nonprofits
summary: Anthropic launched Claude Corps, a fellowship program that will train 1,000 early-career fellows and place them full-time at nonprofits across the United States. The program begins with a $150 million Anthropic commitment and aims to help nonprofits adopt useful Claude workflows while giving fellows practical AI experience.
friendlySummary: Claude Corps will train early-career fellows and place them at nonprofits to help build useful AI workflows. Anthropic says the first commitment is $150 million.
whyItMatters: AI adoption is becoming a workforce and capability-building problem, not only a software rollout. Programs like this may show how smaller organizations can gain AI skills without hiring large technical teams or relying only on consultants.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Nonprofits often lack the budget and staff to experiment with AI deeply. A fellowship model could help them turn AI into practical services and internal tools.
